headline.validated: Every query differentially validated against Trino 481 — same StorageGRID Iceberg warehouse, both engines capped at 12 GB — with DuckDB's official dbgen/dsdgen as an independent data oracle.

stats.label: suites faster than Trino at SF1
suites faster at SF10 too — SSB now at parity

scales.note: Scale factor ≈ dataset size: SF1 ≈ 1 GB, SF10 ≈ 10 GB. Rig: a dedicated 8-core / 31 GB box, both engines capped at 12 GB against the same StorageGRID Iceberg warehouse, Trino 481.
scales.dominant: Faster than Trino at SF1 across seven of eight industry suites — and, as of this run, ahead at SF10 too.
scales.progress: The SF10 crossover is closed. SSB — the one suite we trailed for months (0.53× only a few weeks ago) — is now at parity (20.4s vs 21.4s) after shipping build-side key sets to workers. TPC-H (1.7×) and TPC-DS (1.5×, and now 99/99 correct at SF1) lead at SF10. Two honest exceptions we show rather than hide: TPC-C's sub-second OLTP reads at SF1, where fixed dispatch overhead dominates, and the new bank suite at SF10, where spill still degrades under an 8 GB pool — both being worked.

results.purpose: 22-query decision-support benchmark — the most widely benchmarked SQL workload and SQE's primary comparison target.
99-query decision-support benchmark — larger and harder than TPC-H; pushes joins, window functions, and grouping sets.
Star Schema Benchmark — 13 queries over one fact joined to four dimensions; the shape data warehouses are optimised for. The suite we trailed longest.
43 queries over one wide table — log analytics: heavy string filters, point lookups, and counts. SQE's caching shines here.
Financial-trading workload: date-range filters, point lookups against trade-history, aggregations across many small dimensions.

sf10.note: SF10 diff needs ~14 GB for the 10M-row result — validated test-only

results.purpose: BigBench mixed workload: structured analytics plus JSON and text operations over a retail warehouse schema.
Purpose-built banking workload — customer / KYC / accounts with day-partitioned transactions; time-windowed "last 14 days out of N" scans that lean on partition pruning.

sf10.note: spill stalls under an 8 GB pool — fix in progress

results.purpose: The read SELECTs from the TPC-C OLTP workload — point lookups against small Iceberg tables (SQE runs the read subset, not transactions).

sf1.note: sub-second reads — fixed dispatch overhead dominates

leaps.title: The benchmark that lied
leaps.metric: Differential validation with DuckDB as an independent oracle: caught 16 vacuous TPC-DS queries and a Trino rounding bug (q75).

leaps.title: Our nemesis: TPC-DS q72
leaps.metric: Runtime-filter pushdown drops q72 from 10.7s to 0.77s.

leaps.title: Five layers of caching
leaps.metric: 8.8x faster than Trino across the suite.
